responses.GS(2): The Project Area does not reside within a USDW zone. This assessment is based on the 1982 MOA and the Class II primacy application, the hydrocarbon-bearing zones within the productive limits of named oil fields, as documented in the former DOG “California Oil and Gas Fields” volumes are accepted as exempt aquifers under 40 CFR 146.4 (US EPA, 1982). In the Project Area, the target injection interval consists of the Kione and Forbes Formations, both which are named in California Oil and Gas Fields Volume 3.
responses.GS(4): Isopach maps are provided for the Princeton Canyon (Figure 6), Kione (Figure 7), and Forbes (Figure 8)[cite: 325, 331, 373, 442].
responses.GS(5): Three geologic cross-sections are provided: Strike Section A-A' (Figure 9), and Dip Sections B-B' (Figure 10) and C-C' (Figure 11)[cite: 497, 502, 570, 634].
responses.GS(6): The type log for the project area is from well Section 58 Unit 5 (API 0402120322)[cite: 702, 708, 711].
responses.ES(1): The average ZEI calculated per well is 316’ which is much less than a quarter-mile radius; therefore, the conservative approach of using a quarter-mile radius around the injectors was used to define the AOR. ZEI calculations can be found under the uploaded document ES(1)_ZEI_Calculation. Reservoir properties used for calculations are referenced in the reservoir characteristics table under GS(1) section of the Geologic Study.
responses.ES(2):
See uploaded document ES(2)_Willows-Beehive Bend - AOR Map.
AOR Complete Map displays the AOR for the Kione injection zone in Willows Beehive Bend. The map includes cross section traces and all gas producing wells drilled in Willows Beehive Bend that penetrate the injection zone.
To the knowledge of the operator, there are no underground disposal horizons, mining, or other subsurface industrial activities not associated with oil and gas production within the AORs.
responses.ES(3):
See uploaded document ES(3)_UIC Well List which includes a list of well information including API, total depth, well type, well status, latitude/longitude, and total depth for all wells within the AOR. All wells within the ¼ mile are vertical.
ES(3)_WBDs contains casing diagrams for wells within this UIC project review. Completion interval information is included in the wellbore diagrams. Theoretical Top of Cement (TTOC) is calculated using the annular volume between the outer radius and inner radius of the area filled with cement. Cement volumes can be found in ES(3)_Cement Calculations.

responses.ES(4): No drilling or plugging-and-abandonment programs required to complete this project. All idle wells will be tested or abandoned per the appropriate California Geologic Energy Management (CalGEM) Division regulations. Abandoned wells have been plugged back to meet CalGEM requirements at the time of abandonment. These wells will not have an adverse effect on the project or cause damage to life, health and property.
responses.IP(1): The primary purpose is to dispose of produced water from Sacramento Basin gas fields into the Kione and Forbes formations using well Section 58 Unit 18[cite: 944, 945].
responses.IP(3): The project is expected to last 30 years with a maximum injection rate of 600 bwpd and a cumulative volume of 6,570 Mbbls[cite: 953, 954].
responses.IP(5): Monitoring includes annual fluid injection profile surveys, casing-tubing annulus pressure tests every five years, and continuous SCADA monitoring of pressures and rates[cite: 964, 965, 967].
responses.IP(8): Injection fluid is produced water exclusively sourced from gas production wells within the Sacramento basin[cite: 986].
responses.OI(1): The Maximum Allowable Surface Injection Pressure (MASP) is determined to be 1,623 psi based on a fracture gradient of 0.81 psi/ft[cite: 1171, 1173].
responses.CL(1): Consistent with the Underground Injection Control (UIC) Geologic Energy Management Division Project Information Checklist, dated April 8, 2020, Item CP(1), California Resources Corporation has determined that the project submission is subject to the requirements of California Business and Professions Code sections 6735, 7835, and 7835.1, and as such, the licensed professionals identified below are responsible for all data, interpretations, and calculations that are within the scope of their respective professional licenses.
responses.PO(1): California Resources Corporation is requesting the approval of a Class II Water Disposal Project for the Kione and Forbes Formations in Willows-Beehive Bend Gas Field. The primary purpose of the project is to inject excess produced water from CRC's gas production facilities into the Kione and Forbes Formations within the EPA-approved aquifer exemption boundary (1983). The project will consist of converting one (1) Class II disposal well within the proposed UIC Project Area, which defined as being on CRC-leased property that is on the east flank of the Willows-Beehive Bend anticline, east of Hwy 5, and within the aquifer exemption boundary (Figure 1). The Project Area includes approximately 160 acres or 0.25 square miles.
responses.ES(5):
In December 2025, California Resources Corporation conducted an electronic search to identify the location of water wells within 1-mile of the proposed UIC project boundary. The data from the DWR and GAMA systems are incomplete, and the well types are not specified. The latitude and longitude coordinates are approximate, so the well locations shown on the Department of Water Resources
(DWR) database and the California State Water Resources Control Board Groundwater Ambient Monitoring Assessment Program (GAMA) site maps are imprecise. For wells that included alphanumeric identifiers, the California well numbering system was used for the well locations instead plotting them in the center of the sections.
ES(2) and ES(3) contain a map of the water wells identified as well as Information including type and status for water supply wells.

responses.PO(1):
The primary purpose of Underground Injection Control (UIC) Project 46400396 is to enhance hydrocarbon
recovery by utilizing continuous and cyclic steam injection into the Tulare Formation located on the Main Camp
Lease (Sec 24 T31S R22E MDBM) of the Midway-Sunset Field (Kern County, California), as shown in Figure 1.
The Main Camp Lease is currently operated by Berry Corporation (Berry). The current Project Approval Letter
(PAL) was approved on July 30, 2013.
The current project consists of 67 wells, including 30 steamflood, 5 cyclic, and 32 oil and gas (OG) wells. Berry
is requesting an expansion of UIC Project 46400396 to incorporate the following:
1. 63 new wells (continuous injection and cyclic)
2. converting 26 of the 32 OG producers into cyclic steam status
Upon approval, the project will contain a total of 124 cyclic/steamflood injection wells under the UIC. Berry is
also requesting an update to the PAL to increase the total number of injection wells to 124 and increase the
permitted injection volume to 62,000 BSPD. This development plan is necessary to enhance the ultimate
hydrocarbon recovery of the lease, as operated by Berry.

responses.ES(1):
CalGEM requires an AOR to be completed prior to permitting a Class II injector within the UIC program as per
the federal Safe Drinking Water Act (SDWA) regulations. Pursuant to this requirement, Regulation 40 CFR 146.6
states the minimum fixed radius for an AOR is “1/4 mile from the wellbore unless an approved mathematical
model is used to determine the Zone of Endangering Influence (ZEI).” In accordance with the regulation, a ZEI
calculation was performed for proposed and existing wells (cyclic and continuous injection) using the Neuman
equation (Neuman, 1985).
The ZEI radius for a cyclic-steam producer is calculated to be 220 feet. Most of the heat and fluid injected
during a steam cycle is produced by the same well after it is returned to production. The calculation assumes
that each producer will be cycled twice a year, each year, for a period of 10 years for a total of 20 cycles for the
life of the well. It was further assumed that each cycle will inject 10,000 barrels of steam (Cold Water
Equivalent [CWE]). The cumulative total resultant steam injection volume in a vertical cyclic-steam producer
is calculated to be 200,000 barrels of steam (CWE), representing the maximum cumulative cycle-steam
stimulation injection volume expected per well. The volume is a high estimate for total injected volumes, as
the number of steam cycles performed on cyclic wells is dependent upon response of continuous steamflood
in the reservoir, thus the ZEI is considered as a conservative (i.e. large) estimate.
The ZEI radius for the typical continuous-steam-injector was calculated to be 318 feet. It was estimated that a
typical well will inject a total of 3,192,000 barrels of steam (CWE) into the targeted reservoir sands over a period
of 25 years. The maximum instantaneous steam injection rate per well is estimated to be 500 barrels of steam
(CWE) per day, per well decreasing to 50 barrels of steam (CWE) towards the end of life of the well as seen in
Graph A. The maximum injection rate of 500 barrels of steam per day (BSPD), per well (CWE) represents the
maximum rate of steam to be injected into a typical continuous injection well, while the 50 BSPD end-rate
represents the lowest rate of steam to be injected into a continuous injection well. The 500 barrels of steam
per day (CWE) injection rate would be sustained for approximately one year after which steam injection would
start to be ramped down over time. The ZEI radius for the continuous steam injector and cyclic steam
producer over the corresponding injection periods are reflected in Graph B. The combined ZEI radii for the
proposed and existing continuous steam injectors and cyclic-steam producer are shown in Figure 14 – Project
Development Map.
responses.ES(3):
A comprehensive compendium has been prepared in accordance with the updated Underground Injection
Control Regulations, encompassing all wells identified in the area of review (see Figure 5), including water
supply wells where information was known or publicly available. This compendium includes API numbers or
other identifying information for wells lacking API numbers, along with detailed wellbore paths, total depths,
and depths of completion intervals (presented in Appendix G- ES(3) Wellbore Construction Diagrams).

Furthermore, all data required under Section 1724.7.1 have been compiled and submitted in the form of
graphical casing diagrams in Appendix G - Wellbore Construction Diagrams. This includes all wells within
the area of review that are completed in or penetrate the injection zone or any deeper zone, as well as
directionally drilled wells intersecting the area of review within the zone.

The injection volumes for Berry’s proposed development plan exceed those in the original Project Approval
Letter (PAL) which allows 70 injection wells and 35,000 BSPD, see Appendix F-Current PAL, therefore a
request for an update to the PAL has been proposed in this document. This update includes an increase to
the total number of injection wells and injection volumes - 124 wells and 62,000 BSPD.
Berry will continue to plug and abandon (P&A) wells in the proposed areas as needed to properly manage the
thermal EOR project. The P&A effort will be spread over the life of the development project. Berry is currently
in compliance with the Idle and Long-Term Idle Well (LTIW) program with CalGEM. P&A requirements
associated with each well proposed for an abandonment will be addressed in the abandonment permit prior
to implementation.

responses.IP(1):
The ZEI radius for the typical continuous-steam-injector was calculated to be 318 feet. It was estimated that a
typical well will inject a total of 3,192,000 barrels of steam (CWE) into the targeted reservoir sands over a period
of 25 years. The average steam-injection-rate per well is estimated to be equivalent to 350 barrels of steam
(CWE) per day, decreasing to 50 barrels of steam (CWE) over the life of the well.
responses.IP(2): The list of all wells within project is included in Appendix I- IP(4).
responses.IP(3):
The source of the steam injectate is produced water from the Tulare Formation. A condensed version of recent
geochemical analysis of water samples collected on February 18, 2025 across Berry’s Midway-Sunset assets
is included in Appendix K – IP(8) Injectate Analysis. That document indicate TDS values is 4700 mg/L from
the Tulare at Main Camp (Table 4).
responses.IP(4):
The method of injection for this project is continuous steamflood, a thermal enhanced oil recovery process,
via dedicated continuous steam injector wells. Steam is injected into the reservoir through dedicated tubing
strings below thermal packers. Bottom-hole assembly consists of high-temperature thermal packers which
are set above the injection zone (Figure 17a). This assembly creates an isolation between the casing annulus
and tubing to prevent vertical steam migration. In addition to continuous steam injection, cyclic steam
stimulation (CSS) may be applied to select wells as an initial step to improve injectivity or as a supplemental
recovery technique. Injection wells are equipped with a pressure gauge and monitored daily to ensure MASP
is not exceeded (Figure 17b).
responses.IP(5):
Cathodic protection is in place on the DOT gas line providing fuel gas for generators. No other cathodic
protection measures for the facility, lines or wells are planned at this time, since no significant corrosion has
been identified within the project area.
responses.IP(6):
The Area of Review analysis for the steamflood and cyclic steam project identified 12 wells operated by Berry
within the ZEI Boundary, which do not meet the current CalGEM regulatory requirements for zonal isolation of
the intended injection zone (Title 14- California Code of Regulations, Section 1724.7). The cement calculations
used to screen for potential conduit wells and the potential conduit wells identified are listed within in
Appendix J – IP(5) Cement Calculations and Potential Conduit Wells. This list of wells includes all potential
conduits that fall within this project’s AOR boundary, along with a summary of their current status and a brief
description of how Berry intends to remediate the issue(s). The second list contains wells that will have the
lower portion of their completion interval plugged back upon approval of this project-by-project review.
There has been no indication of steam migration out of zone to date. Berry is proposing a monitoring program
to further ensure the injectate remains confined to the permitted zones. The proposed monitoring plan
consists of the following elements with data submissions to CALGEM as required:
1. Mechanical Integrity Testing (Parts One and Two) in alignment with UIC Regulations 1724.10.2 and
1724.10.3. These regulations stipulate a variety of well integrity and injection monitoring techniques
(temperature surveys, radioactive tracer surveys, casing pressure tests, etc.) that will identify
injection anomalies.
2. Idle well testing according to UIC Regulations 1772.1 that ensures idle wells are mechanically
sound, preventing migration of fluid into non permitted zones. Any indication of steam out zone will
Volume 1 Page 12 of 35
UIC Project 46400396
be followed by additional surveillance and re- abandonment as necessary. This plan adequately
addresses any zonal isolation concerns and monitoring of the project.
3. Idle well fluid levels will be gathered as needed to ensure the wells in question have maintained
mechanical integrity.
4. In addition, Berry will run temperature logs on wells identified as having potential conduits until
remedial actions are completed. These temperature logs will be conducted annually, and any
anomalies observed outside the zone of injection will trigger an escalation of the planned
remediation.
responses.SI(3):
No known water supply well(s) were identified within 500 feet of the UIC Project boundary (AOR radius
boundary).
responses.OI(1):
MASP Determination
The calculated Maximum Allowable Surface Injection Pressure (MASIP) is calculated via the following
equation:
MASIP= (0.95*IG-IFG) * TVD
Where IG is the injection gradient of 0.6 psi/ft
IFG is the injected fluid gradient of 0.02 psi/ft
TVD is the uppermost perforation depth in ft.
The calculated Injection Gradient is multiplied by 0.95 to appropriately account for factors such as a
conservative allowance for friction loss, in accordance with Section 1724.10.3- Maximum Allowable Surface
Injection Pressure.
Injection wells pressure will not exceed its permitted limit for this project. Calculated MASP for wells is
included in Appendix L – OI(1) MASP Calculations.
responses.OI(2):
Letters to Offset Operators
Copies of letters of notification to offset operators can be found in Appendix M – OI(2) Offset Operator
